note: GM behavioral profiles — records are NOT stored here. Live records are injected at runtime from MLB Stats API. Only update behavioral intelligence (trade style, patterns, tendencies). Verify GM names periodically — front offices change.

teams.current_mode: contend — under pressure after 2025 disaster, window is now
teams.fa_appetite: evolved — historically avoided FA but made significant shift in 2025-26 offseason, signed Alonso and made other bold moves after publicly acknowledging he learned hard lessons
teams.trade_style: historically stealth and prospect-focused, but explicitly stated he is adapting and evolving after 2025 failure
teams.prospect_protection: high historically but lowering — farm depleted from 2024 deadline moves; won't gut what remains but shifting toward competing
known_patterns: 2025 was a full disaster — fired manager, went 75-87, traded 9 major leaguers at deadline and got no MLB-ready players back
Explicitly said he wouldn't pursue MLB-ready talent at 2025 deadline because 'you're going to get less talent, less value in return' — fan and media backlash was intense
2025-26 offseason showed genuine behavioral shift — signed Alonso, described as 'taking big swings with more conviction'
Currently 20-24 in 2026, under significant pressure — window is now, not theoretical
Stealth is his natural mode — deals rarely leak in advance
teams.recent_shifts: Most significant shift in his Baltimore tenure — after 2025 disaster he publicly acknowledged the need to adapt. The Alonso signing was his clearest break from prior FA avoidance. At 33-37 in 2026, may face seller pressure despite offseason rebuild efforts.

default_modifier.fits: move that signals commitment to competing, multi-year controlled player, analytically-sound acquisition
default_modifier.contradicts: pure prospect accumulation move, rental that delays competing, overly patient approach that ignores current window
